data.description:
# Browser surface: "Open in default browser" button

Add a small icon button to the browser surface's address-bar accessory cluster that opens the currently loaded URL in the system default browser. Operator-requested ergonomic improvement: there are situations where the in-app c11 browser isn't the right tool (downloads handled by the user's primary browser profile, sign-in flows tied to a specific session, sharing the live tab with someone, etc.) and a pop-out should be one click away.

This is a v1 with no menu wiring, no keyboard shortcut, and no behavioral changes beyond the new button itself.

## Scope (HCI-approved with the operator on 2026-05-26)

| Decision | Value |
|---|---|
| SF Symbol | `arrow.up.right.square` |
| Placement | Right accessory cluster, **between Profile and Theme** |
| Action | `NSWorkspace.shared.open(panel.currentURL)` — no confirmation, c11 surface not closed |
| Disabled state | When `panel.currentURL` is nil OR scheme is `about:` / empty new-tab page |
| Tooltip | `String(localized: "browser.openExternal.help", defaultValue: "Open in Default Browser")` |
| AX identifier | `BrowserOpenExternalButton` |
| AX label | `String(localized: "browser.openExternal.accessibilityLabel", defaultValue: "Open in default browser")` |
| Keyboard shortcut | **None** in v1 |
| Localization | English source via `String(localized:)`; six-locale translation pass (ja/uk/ko/zh-Hans/zh-Hant/ru) over `Resources/Localizable.xcstrings` |

## Where it lands in code

- **File:** `Sources/Panels/BrowserPanelView.swift`
- **Insertion point:** the right accessory `HStack` near line 867:
  ```swift
  HStack(spacing: browserToolbarAccessorySpacing) {
      if shouldShowToolbarImportHintChip { browserImportHintToolbarChip }
      browserProfileButton
      // ← new openInExternalBrowserButton goes HERE (between Profile and Theme)
      browserThemeModeButton
      developerToolsButton
  }
  ```
- **Pattern to follow:** `browserProfileButton` (lines ~979–1005) is the closest structural cousin — same `OmnibarAddressButtonStyle`, same `addressBarButtonSize`, same `cmuxFlatSymbolColorRendering()`, same `devToolsColorOption.color` tinting, same `safeHelp` + `accessibilityIdentifier` wiring.
- **URL source:** `panel.currentURL: URL?` (declared in `Sources/Panels/BrowserPanel.swift` line ~2128). Do NOT read the omnibar text — operator wants the loaded URL, not what they're typing.

## Disabled-state logic

```swift
private var canPopOutCurrentURL: Bool {
    guard let url = panel.currentURL else { return false }
    let scheme = url.scheme?.lowercased() ?? ""
    if scheme.isEmpty || scheme == "about" { return false }
    // Empty new-tab page: c11 may surface the new-tab URL; keep this conservative.
    return true
}
```

Disable via `.disabled(!canPopOutCurrentURL)` + `.opacity(canPopOutCurrentURL ? 1.0 : 0.4)` to match the back/forward pattern at lines 907–908.

## Acceptance criteria

1. **Functional:** with a real URL loaded (e.g. `https://example.com`), clicking the new button launches that URL in the system default browser (whatever macOS reports as default). c11 surface remains open and on the same page.
2. **Disabled correctness:** on a fresh "new tab" browser surface with no URL loaded, the button renders disabled (grey, 40% opacity). Becomes enabled the moment a real URL is navigated.
3. **Tooltip:** hovering shows "Open in Default Browser" (English). The localized string is in `Resources/Localizable.xcstrings` under key `browser.openExternal.help` with a `defaultValue` of "Open in Default Browser".
4. **Accessibility:** the button exposes `BrowserOpenExternalButton` as its accessibility identifier and a localized accessibility label.
5. **Visual fit:** the icon sits between Profile and Theme, same hit size and tint as its neighbors, no layout jump.
6. **Localization sync:** `Resources/Localizable.xcstrings` contains entries for `browser.openExternal.help` and `browser.openExternal.accessibilityLabel` in all six locales (ja, uk, ko, zh-Hans, zh-Hant, ru) plus the English source.
7. **Build green:** `xcodebuild -project GhosttyTabs.xcodeproj -scheme c11-logic -configuration Debug -destination "platform=macOS" build` succeeds. (Test suite untouched — no new tests required per the project's test-quality policy: there's no observable behavior to assert beyond "NSWorkspace.shared.open got called with the URL," which would be a mock-only fake regression test.)
8. **Tagged build inspection:** `./scripts/reload.sh --tag popout-btn` produces a `c11 DEV.app` whose browser surface shows the new button in the expected place. Operator does the final visual check.

## Out of scope (do not do)

- Menu-bar item ("File → Open in Default Browser") — defer.
- Keyboard shortcut — defer (browser surfaces are already congested for Cmd-keys; not worth the risk for v1).
- Right-click context menu entry — defer.
- "Open in specific browser" submenu — system default is fine.
- Closing the c11 surface after pop-out — surprising; don't do it.
- Any change to `panel.currentURL` plumbing or omnibar wiring.

## Workflow

**Mode:** fast-track (single delegator session, inline self-review). One file change + xcstrings + a translator sub-agent pass for the six locales. Self-reviewable surface; no headless `lattice plan-review` / `lattice code-review` needed.

**Branch:** `feat/browser-popout-button` off `origin/main` at the SHA `git rev-parse origin/main` reports at delegator spawn time.

**Translator pass:** after the English strings land in `Localizable.xcstrings`, spawn one translator sub-agent (per the c11 project CLAUDE.md localization pattern) to fill the six locales. Block PR open on that completing.

**Verification before PR:**
1. `xcodebuild ... -scheme c11-logic build` green.
2. Tagged build: `./scripts/reload.sh --tag popout-btn`.
3. Operator-visible smoke: load `https://example.com` in the tagged build's browser surface, click the new button, confirm it opens in macOS default browser.

**PR title:** `BrowserPanelView: add "Open in Default Browser" toolbar button`

**PR body:** brief recap of HCI decisions, before/after screenshot (or ASCII description if screenshot tooling isn't trivial), localization note.
data.title: Browser: Open in default browser toolbar button

data.body: PR #211 merged to main as 58069f5f9 (squash). All 6 CI checks green. HCI spec adhered to. Worktree + local + remote branches cleaned. Operator merge-approved on 2026-05-26.
